message: Add string methods: split, strip, replace

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Test string replace method
+text = "hello world hello"
+
+# Replace hello with hi
+replaced = text.replace("hello", "hi")
+print("Original:")
+print(text)
+print("Replaced:")
+print(replaced)

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Test string split method
+text = "hello,world,python"
+
+# Split by comma
+parts = text.split(",")
+print("Parts length:")
+print(len(parts))
+
+# Verify length is correct
+if len(parts) == 3:
+    print("Split works correctly!")

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Test string strip method
+text = "  hello world  "
+
+# Strip whitespace
+cleaned = text.strip()
+print("Original length:")
+print(len(text))
+print("Stripped length:")
+print(len(cleaned))
+print("Stripped:")
+print(cleaned)

@@ -78,10 +78,17 @@ def generate_call(
                 if self.wrap_primitive_args and not arg_try:
                     # This will be handled specially - return a marker
                     arg_list.append(f"__WRAP_PRIMITIVE__{arg_code}")
+                elif arg_try:
+                    # Argument needs try - wrap it
+                    arg_list.append(f"try {arg_code}")
                 else:
                     arg_list.append(arg_code)
             else:
-                arg_list.append(arg_code)
+                # Handle generic arguments
+                if arg_try:
+                    arg_list.append(f"try {arg_code}")
+                else:
+                    arg_list.append(arg_code)
 
         args_str = ", ".join(arg_list)
         call_code = f"runtime.{self.runtime_type}.{self.runtime_fn}({args_str})"
@@ -112,6 +119,30 @@ def generate_call(

         arg_types=[],
     ),

+    ),

     # List methods
